justification: All pass criteria met: Both queues (order.fulfillment, order.fulfillment.dlq) identified by name. Both env vars (RABBITMQ_URL, FULFILLMENT_DLQ_NAME) identified by name. Escalations routed to Coach K (rows 1-4 all route through Coach K). new_infrastructure_required section fully populated with new_queues_topics and new_env_vars. Verdict is NOT READY. Observability noted as partially present (structured logging, p99 latency) — Pippen correctly identifies gaps without false-positiving on what exists.

observations.text: Infrastructure gap identification is thorough — goes beyond the minimum by also flagging DLQ routing policy, exchange bindings, and queue durability settings as undefined
new_infrastructure_required section is exceptionally detailed with a table covering env vars, services, queues, config changes, and provisioning status
Escalations correctly route through Coach K as the hub, with Coach K then directing to MJ, Infra team, and Bird as appropriate — this matches the expected routing pattern
Verdict is unambiguously NOT READY with clear reasoning that unprovisioned infrastructure is a hard blocker
Goes beyond minimum by identifying additional risks: no message producer, unknown idempotency, missing reconnection logic, unbounded prefetch
Correctly notes observability is partially adequate (structured logging present, p99 exported) rather than false-positiving on it — the blocker is infrastructure, not observability

Escalation routing adds Bird and Infra team as secondary targets beyond Coach K — while Coach K is correctly the primary route, the rubric expected escalation solely to Coach K rather than Coach K as a pass-through to other agents
